perturbation.original_text: 6. Value Creation Committee and Other Matters. (a) At the March Board Meeting, the Board shall establish a new committee of the Board (the "Value Creation Committee"), whose purpose will be to evaluate all options for enhancing shareholder value, including by (i) engaging Bank of America Merrill Lynch, or if Bank of America Merrill Lynch is unavailable or the Company is unable to reach acceptable terms with Merrill Lynch Bank of America despite using reasonable best efforts to do so, another investment bank mutually agreeable to the Company and JANA, as promptly as practicable, to review all options for enhancing value, including by conducting a strategic review of the Company's business, operations and capital structure; (ii) engaging a cost consultant, mutually agreeable to the Company and JANA, to conduct a cost review commencing with the completion of the investment banker review described in clause (ii) above; and (iii) reviewing the Company's management compensation structure to enhance alignment with shareholder value creation. There shall be four (4) members of the Value Creation Committee, two (2) of whom shall be JANA Nominees chosen by JANA, and the Board shall not increase the size of the Value Creation Committee until the 2015 Annual Meeting, if still in existence at such time. In the event of the replacement as set forth in Section 3(c) of any JANA Nominee appointed to the Value Creation Committee pursuant to this Section 5, his or her successor shall be promptly appointed to the committee seat vacated by such former director to serve until the 2015 Annual Meeting
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Value Creation Committee and Other Matters. (a) At the March Board Meeting, the Board shall establish a new committee of the Board (the "Value Creation Committee"), whose purpose will be to evaluate all options for enhancing shareholder value, including by (i) engaging Bank of America Merrill Lynch, or if Bank of America Merrill Lynch is unavailable or the Company is unable to reach acceptable terms with Merrill Lynch Bank of America despite using reasonable best efforts to do so, another investment bank solely selected by the Company, as promptly as practicable, to review all options for enhancing value, including by conducting a strategic review of the Company's business, operations and capital structure; (ii) engaging a cost consultant, solely selected by the Company, to conduct a cost review commencing with the completion of the investment banker review described in clause (ii) above; and (iii) reviewing the Company's management compensation structure to enhance alignment with shareholder value creation. There shall be four (4) members of the Value Creation Committee, two (2) of whom shall be JANA Nominees chosen by JANA, and the Board shall not increase the size of the Value Creation Committee until the 2015 Annual Meeting, if still in existence at such time. In the event of the replacement as set forth in Section 3(c) of any JANA Nominee appointed to the Value Creation Committee pursuant to this Section 5, his or her successor shall be promptly appointed to the committee seat vacated by such former director to serve until the 2015 Annual Meeting
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for mutual agreement between the Company and JANA on the selection of the investment bank and cost consultant for the Value Creation Committee. This contradicts the initial intent of cooperation between the parties, especially given the committee's purpose is to enhance shareholder value for both parties. This alteration leads to uncertainty about whether JANA's interests are adequately represented and could lead to disputes over the strategic direction of the committee's work. This impacts the section of the document that describes the cooperation period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Cooperation. (a) JANA agrees that, from the date of this Agreement until the earliest of (i) the date that is thirty (30) calendar days prior to any applicable deadline by which a shareholder must give notice to the Company of its intention to nominate a director for election at or bring other business before the 2015 Annual Meeting under the Company's By­Laws and (ii) any material breach of this Agreement by the Company (provided that the Company shall have three (3) business days following written notice from JANA of material breach to remedy such material breach if capable of remedy) (such period, the "Cooperation Period"), neither it nor any of its Affiliates or Associates will in any manner, directly or indirectly, make, or cause to be made, or in any way encourage any other person to make or cause to be made, any statement or announcement that relates to and constitutes an ad hominem attack on, or relates to and otherwise disparages, the Company, any of its officers or directors or any person who has served as an officer or director of the Company, including: (i) in any document or report filed with or furnished to the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") or any other governmental agency, (ii) in any press release or other publicly available format or (iii) to any journalist or member of the media (including without limitation, in a television, radio, newspaper or magazine interview), or otherwise; provided, that if the Company makes any material announcement prior to the March Board Meeting, JANA will be permitted to make objective statements that solely reflect JANA's view, as a shareholder, with respect to such announcement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated mutual agreement between the Company and JANA for selecting the investment bank and cost consultant. The changed text removes this requirement, granting the Company sole discretion. This directly contradicts the 'Cooperation Period' outlined in Section 7(a), which emphasizes cooperation between JANA and the Company. By removing JANA's input on key decisions, the changed text undermines the spirit and intent of cooperation during the Cooperation Period, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: (b) The Company hereby agrees that it will not make any acquisitions during the Cooperation Period, except for ordinary course acquisitions individually under $10 million (not to exceed $30 million in the aggregate) or acquisitions that a majority of the JANA Nominees have recommended.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) The Company hereby agrees that it will not make any acquisitions during the Cooperation Period, except for ordinary course acquisitions individually under $20 million (not to exceed $60 million in the aggregate).
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by removing the JANA Nominee's approval for company aquisitions. The removal of JANA nominee influence now contradicts the company's representation of cooperation and is ambiguous in enforcing the 'Cooperation Period' of the agreement as the company can make aquisitions without JANA nominee oversight.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Cooperation. (a) JANA agrees that, from the date of this Agreement until the earliest of (i) the date that is thirty (30) calendar days prior to any applicable deadline by which a shareholder must give notice to the Company of its intention to nominate a director for election at or bring other business before the 2015 Annual Meeting under the Company's By­Laws and (ii) any material breach of this Agreement by the Company (provided that the Company shall have three (3) business days following written notice from JANA of material breach to remedy such material breach if capable of remedy) (such period, the "Cooperation Period"), neither it nor any of its Affiliates or Associates will in any manner, directly or indirectly, make, or cause to be made, or in any way encourage any other person to make or cause to be made, any statement or announcement that relates to and constitutes an ad hominem attack on, or relates to and otherwise disparages, the Company, any of its officers or directors or any person who has served as an officer or director of the Company, including: (i) in any document or report filed with or furnished to the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") or any other governmental agency, (ii) in any press release or other publicly available format or (iii) to any journalist or member of the media (including without limitation, in a television, radio, newspaper or magazine interview), or otherwise; provided, that if the Company makes any material announcement prior to the March Board Meeting, JANA will be permitted to make objective statements that solely reflect JANA's view, as a shareholder, with respect to such announcement.
perturbation.justification: The original text included the requirement that a majority of the JANA Nominees approve acquisitions exceeding the stipulated financial thresholds. The changed text removes this requirement, giving the Company greater autonomy in making acquisitions during the 'Cooperation Period' without JANA Nominee oversight. This directly contradicts the intent of Section 7(a), which outlines the 'Cooperation Period' and JANA's commitment to refrain from actions that disparage the company. The removal of JANA's approval power undermines the spirit of cooperation intended in the original agreement, creating an inconsistency in the enforcement of the Cooperation Period as outlined in the Contradicted Text, thus causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Notwithstanding any other Section in this Agreement and without limiting any other remedies the Company may have in law or equity, in the event that JANA (or any Affiliate or Associate of JANA) fails to perform or otherwise fulfill its obligations set forth in Section 7 in any material respect, and shall not have remedied such failure or non-fulfillment if capable of being remedied or fulfilled within three (3) business days following written notice from the Company of such failure or non-fulfillment, the Company shall not be required to perform or fulfill its obligations set forth in Sections 3, 4, 5 or 7 and the JANA Nominees shall each promptly tender their resignation as a member of the Board effective immediately upon its acceptance by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Notwithstanding any other Section in this Agreement and without limiting any other remedies the Company may have in law or equity, in the event that JANA (or any Affiliate or Associate of JANA) fails to perform or otherwise fulfill its obligations set forth in Section 7 in any material respect, and shall not have remedied such failure or non-fulfillment if capable of being remedied or fulfilled within ten (10) business days following written notice from the Company of such failure or non-fulfillment, the Company shall not be required to perform or fulfill its obligations set forth in Sections 3, 4, 5 or 7 and the JANA Nominees shall each promptly tender their resignation as a member of the Board effective immediately upon its acceptance by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text extends the remedy period for JANA's breach from three to ten business days. This contradicts the original intention of swiftly addressing breaches to maintain the cooperation framework. The ambiguity arises as to which timeframe is legally binding, impacting the Company's ability to enforce the agreement promptly. The longer period can be argued to undermine the purpose of section 7 which is intended to have quick co-operation between the two parites. This impacts the Company's ability to co-operate with the JANA Nominees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Cooperation. (a) JANA agrees that, from the date of this Agreement until the earliest of (i) the date that is thirty (30) calendar days prior to any applicable deadline by which a shareholder must give notice to the Company of its intention to nominate a director for election at or bring other business before the 2015 Annual Meeting under the Company's By­Laws and (ii) any material breach of this Agreement by the Company (provided that the Company shall have three (3) business days following written notice from JANA of material breach to remedy such material breach if capable of remedy) (such period, the "Cooperation Period"), neither it nor any of its Affiliates or Associates will in any manner, directly or indirectly, make, or cause to be made, or in any way encourage any other person to make or cause to be made, any statement or announcement that relates to and constitutes an ad hominem attack on, or relates to and otherwise disparages, the Company, any of its officers or directors or any person who has served as an officer or director of the Company, including: (i) in any document or report filed with or furnished to the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") or any other governmental agency, (ii) in any press release or other publicly available format or (iii) to any journalist or member of the media (including without limitation, in a television, radio, newspaper or magazine interview), or otherwise; provided, that if the Company makes any material announcement prior to the March Board Meeting, JANA will be permitted to make objective statements that solely reflect JANA's view, as a shareholder, with respect to such announcement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the cure period for JANA's breach from three to ten business days. This directly contradicts the three-day cure period provided to JANA in Section 7(a) of the agreement. The change creates an inconsistency regarding the time frame JANA has to remedy a breach, creating a contradiction within the contract. The contradiction is strong because the Company and JANA have two different remedy periods for a 'material breach' in Section 7.
